26.reason: [The function does not handle the case where root is not NULL and both recursive calls are skipped due to missing else after the first if(root==NULL). This leads to unnecessary traversal and incorrect height calculation.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The function does not handle the case where root is not NULL and both recursive calls are skipped due to missing else after the first if(root==NULL). This leads to unnecessary traversal and incorrect height calculation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The function does not handle the case where root is not NULL but both recursive conditions are skipped (e.g., when a == root->num and the else if is taken, but after incrementing dqshendu, it still recurses; however, more critically, after creating a new node, the code continues to check the if/else conditions, leading to incorrect recursion on a just-allocated node.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [The function does not handle the case where root is not NULL and both recursive calls are skipped due to missing else; also, dqshendu is passed by value so incrementing it doesn't affect caller's depth correctly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [The function does not handle the case where root is not NULL but both recursive conditions fail (e.g., when a == root->num and right subtree already exists); also, after creating a new node, it continues to check insertion conditions, which may cause incorrect traversal or double insertion logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The function does not handle the case when root is not NULL and both recursive calls are skipped (e.g., when a == root->num and right subtree already exists), leading to potential infinite recursion or incorrect tree structure. Also, the logic for updating depth is flawed because dqshendu is passed by value, so increments in deeper calls don't affect parent calls.]
